concerns.text: FR-008 requires statistical significance testing (p-values, ANOVA) on census data. For a complete enumeration of prime knots ≤13 crossings, statistical inference is conceptually invalid—there is no sampling distribution. Effect sizes alone are appropriate; p-values should be removed or explicitly marked as 'not applicable for census data' rather than 'supplementary for reporting completeness'.

FR-005 acknowledges braid index ≤ crossing number as a 'mathematical constraint' but still requires joint regression with VIF assessment. This is not multicollinearity—it is a definitional dependency. Regression coefficients for braid index cannot be interpreted as independent effects when bounded by another predictor. The spec should either: (1) use braid index alone as predictor, or (2) acknowledge that joint regression answers a different question than 'joint predictive power'.

Assumptions section requires 'Power analysis will be documented to confirm sufficient power to detect medium effect sizes' for census data. Power analysis applies to sampling designs, not complete enumerations. This should be removed or reframed as 'sample size documentation for stratified groups' since all available knots are included by definition.

SC-011 requires ANOVA testing with effect sizes and p-values for alternating vs. non-alternating comparisons. For census data, group differences are exact descriptive facts, not statistical inferences. ANOVA assumptions (normality, equal variance) are irrelevant when computing exact population parameters. Replace with descriptive comparison metrics (mean difference, variance ratio) without significance testing.

FR-014 states conclusions apply to 'hyperbolic prime knots' but the research question in Idea MD also says 'hyperbolic prime knots' while some spec language (e.g., SC-001) says 'prime knots' without qualification. Ensure consistent terminology throughout to avoid scope ambiguity between 'all prime knots' vs 'hyperbolic prime knots only'.
